title: Sloanea obtusifolia
taxonomy: Sloanea obtusifolia is classified within the family Erysomataceae, but some sources suggest it belongs to the family Sapindaceae. This classification may vary depending on different botanical sources and classifications. Some sources consider Sloanea obtusifolia to be part of the Sapindales order. The genus Sloanea is also part of the Sapindales order. Further research is needed to determine the most accurate classification for Sloanea obtusifolia.
uses: Sloanea obtusifolia is utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's extracts have been researched for their antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Sloanea obtusifolia may also exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which could contribute to its medicinal value. Furthermore, it is believed to possess wound-healing properties and is sometimes used to treat skin conditions. Research into Sloanea obtusifolia's medicinal uses is ongoing to fully understand its potential applications.

intro: Sloanea obtusifolia is a medicinal plant species commonly found in the tropical regions of Southeast Asia, known for its potential health benefits and traditional uses. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Sloanea obtusifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sloanea obtusifolia is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
